+title: 'Test isolation bug: skill/agent/workspace deployment pollutes the real source tree'
+---
+## What
+One or more tests deploy skills / agents / workspace files into the **real repository source tree** instead of an isolated temp dir. Observed pollution (all untracked, now cleaned up manually):
+
+- Repo-root directories `swissarmyhammer-cli/` and `shelltool-cli/` — full mirdan deployments: `.claude/skills`, `.claude/agents`, `.zed/skills`, `.github/copilot/skills`, `.agents/*/AGENT.md`, `.skills/*/SKILL.md`, `.sah/sah.yaml` (`model: GLM-5.0`), `.sah/mcp.log`, `.shell/mcp.log`, `mirdan-lock.json`.
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/.{claude,github,skills,zed}` and `apps/shelltool-cli/.{claude,github,skills,zed}` — same kind of deployment landing in the crate manifest dirs.
+- Tracked files overwritten in place: `apps/code-context-cli/.skills/code-context/SKILL.md`, `apps/code-context-cli/.skills/lsp/SKILL.md` (reverted via `git checkout`).
+
+The repo-root `swissarmyhammer-cli/` / `shelltool-cli/` names are the **old** crate locations (crates moved to `apps/` in `a70af2f95`). The crate-name-shaped dirs at root strongly suggest a test deploys to a path derived from the crate name / a stale relative path / process CWD rather than an explicit temp root.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] The test(s) that deploy skills/agents/workspace/mirdan files without temp-dir isolation are identified and fixed to use an isolated temp dir (or a `CurrentDirGuard` scoped to a temp dir).
+- [ ] Running the affected crates' test suites leaves `git status` clean — no `.claude/.github/.skills/.zed/.sah/.shell` dirs, no `mirdan-lock.json`, no root `swissarmyhammer-cli/`/`shelltool-cli/` dirs, no modified tracked `SKILL.md` files.
+- [ ] If deployment code silently defaults to `current_dir()` in a way tests can trigger against the repo, that is hardened or the test is given an explicit root.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] After the fix, run the affected crates' tests and assert (in the test or by inspection) that deployment targets a temp dir.
+- [ ] `cargo test` for the affected crate(s) is green and the working tree stays clean.
\ No newline at end of file

+- [x] After the fix, run the affected crates' tests and assert (in the test or by inspection) that deployment targets a temp dir.
+- [x] `cargo test` for the affected crate(s) is green and the working tree stays clean. #bug
+
+## Implementation Notes
+
+### Root cause
+mirdan's deployment functions resolve their targets from **process-relative paths**:
+- `mirdan::store::skill_store_dir(false)` returns the relative path `.skills` (joined against process CWD).
+- `mirdan::agents::agent_project_skill_dir` returns each agent's `project_path` from `agents_default.yaml`, which are relative (`.claude/skills`, `.zed/skills`, `.github/copilot/skills`).
+- `swissarmyhammer-cli`'s `load_project_lockfile()` and `ModelManager::ensure_config_structure` both use `std::env::current_dir()` and write `mirdan-lock.json` / `.sah/sah.yaml` there.
+
+This is correct for production (a CLI is rooted at the user's CWD by design). The bug is that several **unit and integration tests called this real deployment code without first isolating CWD**. During `cargo test`/`nextest` the CWD is the crate manifest dir, so the deployment wrote `.skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.zed/skills/`, `.github/copilot/skills/`, and `.sah/sah.yaml` straight into the crate source tree — including overwriting the tracked `apps/code-context-cli/.skills/*/SKILL.md` files.
+
+### Offending tests found and how each was isolated
+Each offending unit test now chdir's into a fresh `tempfile::TempDir` for the duration of the call via a local `isolated_deploy_dir()` helper that wraps `swissarmyhammer_common::test_utils::CurrentDirGuard` (panic-safe, restores CWD on drop, serialized by the existing global CWD mutex).
+
+- `apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/skill.rs` — `test_deploy_shell_skill_returns_valid_result`, `test_shelltool_skill_deployment_init`, `test_shelltool_skill_deployment_deinit` (call `deploy_shell_skill()` / `ShelltoolSkillDeployment::init`/`deinit` → `deploy_skill_to_agents`/`uninstall_skill`).
+- `apps/kanban-cli/src/commands/skill.rs` — `test_deploy_kanban_skill_returns_valid_result`, `test_kanban_skill_deployment_init`, `test_kanban_skill_deployment_deinit` (same pattern for the `kanban` skill).
+- `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/skill.rs` — `test_run_skill_returns_valid_exit_code` (calls `run_skill()` → deploys `code-context` and `lsp`; this was the test overwriting the tracked `.skills/*/SKILL.md`).
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/skill.rs` — `test_skill_deployment_init_returns_one_result`, `test_skill_deployment_deinit_returns_one_result` (call `SkillDeployment::init`/`deinit` → deploys all builtin skills + writes `mirdan-lock.json`).
+
+Two integration tests in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli` ran the `sah model use` subprocess without an explicit working directory, so the subprocess inherited the test process's CWD (the crate manifest dir) and wrote `.sah/sah.yaml` into the source tree:
+
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/model_commands.rs::test_model_use_builtin_model` — switched from `run_model_command` (no `current_dir`) to `run_model_command_in_dir(..., env.temp_dir())` using the `IsolatedTestEnvironment`'s temp dir.
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/model_performance_edge_casess.rs::test_invalid_yaml_recovery` — changed the `model use claude-code` call from `working_dir: None` to `Some(&temp_dir)` (the test's existing isolated temp dir).
+
+### Design note
+mirdan's deploy functions are not themselves buggy — defaulting to CWD-relative paths is the intended production behavior. The fix is in the tests (explicit isolation), which is the correct boundary: the tests were exercising production deployment against the live repo. No `#[ignore]` was used; all tests still run, just isolated.
+
+### Verification
+After the fix, running every offending test (all 9 skill unit tests across the 4 CLI crates + both model integration tests) leaves `git status --porcelain` showing only the intended source edits — no `.claude/.github/.skills/.zed/.sah/.shell` dirs, no `mirdan-lock.json`, no root crate-named dirs, no modified tracked `SKILL.md`. `cargo test` for the touched crates' bin/lib units (435 + 106 + 85 + 46) and model integration modules (19 + 10 + 16) is green single-threaded; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` for all four crates is clean.
+
+A pre-existing, unrelated parallel-execution flake in the model integration tests (unsynchronized `HOME` env mutation) was discovered and filed as a separate task (`01KRTYAJDRZ48ECQFF1BR48RBP`); it is not source-tree pollution and is out of scope for this bug.
\ No newline at end of file

+A pre-existing, unrelated parallel-execution flake in the model integration tests (unsynchronized `HOME` env mutation) was discovered and filed as a separate task (`01KRTYAJDRZ48ECQFF1BR48RBP`); it is not source-tree pollution and is out of scope for this bug.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-17 14:32)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:616,630` — `test_skill_deployment_init_returns_one_result` and `test_skill_deployment_deinit_returns_one_result` use `isolated_deploy_dir()` (→ `CurrentDirGuard`) to mutate process-global CWD but carry no `#[serial_test::serial(cwd)]` attribute. The rest of this crate consistently puts every CWD-touching test into the named `cwd` serialization group: `src/commands/registry.rs:71/100/117` (also `CurrentDirGuard`-based), `src/commands/model/use_command.rs:295`, and `src/commands/doctor/checks.rs:637/669` (raw `std::env::set_current_dir`). The global `CURRENT_DIR_LOCK` mutex inside `CurrentDirGuard` only serializes against *other* `CurrentDirGuard` users — it does NOT serialize against the raw `set_current_dir` calls in `doctor/checks.rs` and `use_command.rs`, which rely solely on the `serial(cwd)` group for isolation. A skill-deployment test can therefore run concurrently with a doctor/model test and the two corrupt each other's CWD. Add `#[serial_test::serial(cwd)]` to both skill tests so they join the crate-wide `cwd` group. `serial_test` is already a dev-dependency (`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/Cargo.toml:118`).
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:217,246,259` — `test_deploy_shell_skill_returns_valid_result`, `test_shelltool_skill_deployment_init`, `test_shelltool_skill_deployment_deinit` mutate global CWD via `isolated_deploy_dir()` but lack `#[serial]`. The established pattern in this same crate pairs `CurrentDirGuard` with `#[serial]` precisely for this reason — see `src/logging.rs:104-109` (doc comment: "uses `#[serial]` to keep the `CurrentDirGuard`'s CWD change from racing with other tests in this crate") and `src/main.rs:225` / `src/commands/serve.rs:538`. Without `#[serial]` these tests fall outside that serialization group and can interleave with `#[serial]` CWD tests. Add `#[serial]` (import `use serial_test::serial;` as `logging.rs` does); `serial_test` is already a dev-dependency (`apps/shelltool-cli/Cargo.toml:43`).
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:227,253,266` — `test_deploy_kanban_skill_returns_valid_result`, `test_kanban_skill_deployment_init`, `test_kanban_skill_deployment_deinit` mutate global CWD via `isolated_deploy_dir()` but lack `#[serial]`. This crate already documents the required convention: `src/commands/doctor.rs:265` states "Uses `CurrentDirGuard` + `#[serial]` per `feedback_test_isolation.md` so this test cannot race with other tests that read or mutate CWD" (see `doctor.rs:268`, `logging.rs:79`, `serve.rs:538`). Add `#[serial_test::serial]` to the three skill tests; `serial_test` is already a dev-dependency (`apps/kanban-cli/Cargo.toml:43`).
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:236` — `test_run_skill_returns_valid_exit_code` mutates global CWD via `isolated_deploy_dir()` but lacks `#[serial]`. Other CWD-touching tests in this crate use it: `src/logging.rs:105` (`#[serial]` + `CurrentDirGuard`). Without it this test can race other tests in the binary on the process-wide CWD. Add `#[serial_test::serial]`; `serial_test` is already a dev-dependency (`apps/code-context-cli/Cargo.toml:46`).
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:222`, `apps/kanban-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:232`, `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:242`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:622,633` — the isolated deploy tests only assert "does not panic" / `results.len() == 1` / exit code in {0,1}; none assert that the temp dir actually received the deployed `.skills/`/`.claude/skills` files. The task's Tests checkbox ("assert in the test or by inspection that deployment targets a temp dir") is satisfied by inspection (the Verification note), so this is not blocking — but a positive assertion (e.g. `temp.path().join(".skills").join(SKILL_NAME).join("SKILL.md").exists()` after a successful deploy) would make the isolation self-verifying and turn the test into a true regression guard rather than a no-panic smoke test.
+- [ ] Implementation Notes "Verification" — verification was performed "green single-threaded". Single-threaded runs cannot exercise the concurrency these tests are exposed to under the default parallel runner (`cargo test` and `nextest` both run tests in a binary in parallel; `.config/nextest.toml` does not serialize these four CLI packages). After adding the `#[serial]` / `serial(cwd)` attributes, re-verify with the default parallel runner so the verification reflects how the suite actually runs in CI.
\ No newline at end of file
